title: spermacoce arvensis
intro: Spermacoce arvensis, also known as Woodruff or Wild Sweet Woodruff, is a perennial herb native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic and antispasmodic properties, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. The plant has been used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and anxiety, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation and improving sleep quality. Spermacoce arvensis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often used in herbal remedies to promote relaxation and calmness.
